task: Continue implementation in the existing compiler repair workflow and complete the real work beyond the scaffold. The previous pass removed the bad recursive/direct-to-bytecode parser but left minimal parse/HIR/lowering that emits RETURN0. Now implement real compiler behavior end-to-end on the new architecture. Strict constraints: no recursive parser emit, parser builds parse products only, semantic phase builds/resolves HIR, lowering alone emits bytecode, nesting lives in typed products/stacks, no direct-to-bytecode parser resurrection. Focus on making real source programs compile and execute again through parse products -> HIR -> lowering: at minimum restore and extend the previously supported language slice (return, local decl/assign, integer/bool/nil expressions, arithmetic + - * / % // &, numeric for if safe) through the new phases, with typed parse nodes, HIR nodes, and lowering. If feasible, add strings/constants/globals or simple if/while, but do not compromise architecture. Remove/minimize placeholder RETURN0-only lowering. Use only safe grep guards and tiny capped smoke checks; do not run broad or memory-heavy tests. Report concrete grammar now parsed into products, HIR now built, bytecode now lowered, files changed, checks run, and exact remaining gaps.
